---
schema: verdi.deviation/v1
covers: 4fbf19b247fe0d06c3f5e4ec3a7019d406f08ff6
findings:
  - { id: judged-f1, kind: judged, text: "ac-3/co-1 promise the empty-bucket claim is 'driven against a fixture store' (ac-3's evidence clause: 'Playwright drives a fixture store with at least one empty bucket'), but the implementing e2e (43-home-status-glance.spec.ts, third test) drives a storeless, in-process workbench whose HomeDeps.Index is canned Go data (cmd/e2eharness/emptyglance.go: an os.MkdirTemp empty root, 'no git, no store on disk', refindex.ComputeIndex bypassed entirely). The renderer downstream of the injected seam is proven, but no Playwright proof exists that a REAL store shaped with an empty bucket flows through index computation to the rendered heading/zero-count/'None.' — the hermeticity intent of co-1 is honored while its 'fixture store, never live data' letter is substituted with a canned index, an evidence-shape divergence a boundary diff cannot see because HomeDeps.Index is a declared seam. (confidence 0.50)" }
  - { id: judged-f2, kind: judged, text: "ac-3 states 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:80-83) returns before writing anything — no \u003csection data-testid=\"home-glance\"\u003e, no bucket headings, no counts — so a failed-index render of GET / carries zero glance structure. The implementation resolves ac-3's tension with co-2 ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the entire section rather than, e.g., rendering the fixed shell without fabricated groups; that carve-out to ac-3's universal quantifier is self-adjudicated in a code comment rather than declared anywhere in the spec record, and the e2e suite never drives GET / under an index failure to pin which reading is contractual (only a unit test, TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, does). (confidence 0.30)" }
digest: sha256:1937c6fc1c929331001377550b8719ea6e2270cf99bc7b44eba29303068eb213
integrity: sha256:44ff3186493333a17af71d028781be508fe94ff5245c7953669df45a4c1043ec
judge_integrity: { stdin_b64: , raw_result: "```json\n{\"findings\":[{\"id\":\"f1\",\"text\":\"ac-3/co-1 promise the empty-bucket claim is 'driven against a fixture store' (ac-3's evidence clause: 'Playwright drives a fixture store with at least one empty bucket'), but the implementing e2e (43-home-status-glance.spec.ts, third test) drives a storeless, in-process workbench whose HomeDeps.Index is canned Go data (cmd/e2eharness/emptyglance.go: an os.MkdirTemp empty root, 'no git, no store on disk', refindex.ComputeIndex bypassed entirely). The renderer downstream of the injected seam is proven, but no Playwright proof exists that a REAL store shaped with an empty bucket flows through index computation to the rendered heading/zero-count/'None.' — the hermeticity intent of co-1 is honored while its 'fixture store, never live data' letter is substituted with a canned index, an evidence-shape divergence a boundary diff cannot see because HomeDeps.Index is a declared seam.\",\"confidence\":0.5},{\"id\":\"f2\",\"text\":\"ac-3 states 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:80-83) returns before writing anything — no \u003csection data-testid=\\\"home-glance\\\"\u003e, no bucket headings, no counts — so a failed-index render of GET / carries zero glance structure. The implementation resolves ac-3's tension with co-2 ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the entire section rather than, e.g., rendering the fixed shell without fabricated groups; that carve-out to ac-3's universal quantifier is self-adjudicated in a code comment rather than declared anywhere in the spec record, and the e2e suite never drives GET / under an index failure to pin which reading is contractual (only a unit test, TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, does).\",\"confidence\":0.3}]}\n```" }
provenance: { generator: verdi-align, version: v0, inputs: [spec/home-status-glance@4fbf19b247fe0d06c3f5e4ec3a7019d406f08ff6, spec/home-status-glance@28024ab6c2631d07f449df8178b6c26b70c14787], digest: sha256:1937c6fc1c929331001377550b8719ea6e2270cf99bc7b44eba29303068eb213, integrity: sha256:44ff3186493333a17af71d028781be508fe94ff5245c7953669df45a4c1043ec }

# Alignment report

## Computed

(none)

### Boundary diff vs acceptance baseline

(no impacted services)

### Diagram alignment

- (no accepted proposals)
- (no illustrative diagrams in this spec's body)

## Judged

- **judged-f1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-3/co-1 promise the empty-bucket claim is 'driven against a fixture store' (ac-3's evidence clause: 'Playwright drives a fixture store with at least one empty bucket'), but the implementing e2e (43-home-status-glance.spec.ts, third test) drives a storeless, in-process workbench whose HomeDeps.Index is canned Go data (cmd/e2eharness/emptyglance.go: an os.MkdirTemp empty root, 'no git, no store on disk', refindex.ComputeIndex bypassed entirely). The renderer downstream of the injected seam is proven, but no Playwright proof exists that a REAL store shaped with an empty bucket flows through index computation to the rendered heading/zero-count/'None.' — the hermeticity intent of co-1 is honored while its 'fixture store, never live data' letter is substituted with a canned index, an evidence-shape divergence a boundary diff cannot see because HomeDeps.Index is a declared seam. (confidence 0.50)
- **judged-f2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: ac-3 states 'all three buckets are structurally present on every render of GET /', but on an index-computation failure writeGlanceSection (internal/workbench/glance.go:80-83) returns before writing anything — no <section data-testid="home-glance">, no bucket headings, no counts — so a failed-index render of GET / carries zero glance structure. The implementation resolves ac-3's tension with co-2 ('never renders partial or fabricated groups when the index itself failed') by omitting the entire section rather than, e.g., rendering the fixed shell without fabricated groups; that carve-out to ac-3's universal quantifier is self-adjudicated in a code comment rather than declared anywhere in the spec record, and the e2e suite never drives GET / under an index failure to pin which reading is contractual (only a unit test, TestWriteGlanceSection_IndexFailure_RendersNothing, does). (confidence 0.30)
